full_text:
Spatially-derived fibroblast signatures onto scRNA-seq-data – Correlation plots, part 2 –

Kürşat Birgin
Meeting, 03.02.2026

1

Aims

Does samples/patients with more exhausted CD8 or CD4 also have more TSI or Stroma activity?

Does samples/patients with for instance high tumor TROP2 expression tend to be associated with high TSI or Stroma scores in fibroblasts?

X-axis: Average UCell score for CD4, CD8, Expression TIGIT on CD8 or TROP2 on tumor (per patient/sample) 

Y-axis: Average UCell score for TSI or Stroma in Fibroblasts (per patient/sample)

Dataset

scRNA-seq dataset from Inga-Maria (Launonen et al.)

Spatially-derived signatures (TSI, Stroma, CD4, and CD8) from Nika

Gene expression information for TIGIT and TROP2 from the scRNA-seq dataset

Here: IDS/PDS combined, couldn’t find the time yet to run separately

We have 29 patients in total (so, 29 dots for the per patient correlation plots):

22 patients have 2 samples
7 patients have 1 sample

51 samples from 29 patients

Patients with 2 samples contribute 2 dots to the per sample correlation plots
Patients with 1 sample contribute 1 dot to the per sample correlation plots

Basic workflow

* It compares the rank/order of values rather than the exact numbers, so it’s robust to outliers. 
 Our gene expression and UCell scores aren’t normally distributed or strictly linear, so Spearman tells us whether higher values generally go with higher (or lower) values.

Correlation plots per sample

Spearman's rank correlation rho

data: cd8_ex_df$CD8_Ex_avg and cd8_ex_df$Stroma_avg
S = 5498, p-value = 3.832e-09
alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
sample estimates:
 rho 
0.7359904

Spearman's rank correlation rho

data: cd8_ex_df$CD8_Ex_avg and cd8_ex_df$TSI_avg
S = 26806, p-value = 0.04352
alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
sample estimates:
 rho 
-0.2872029

Rho interpretation: Strong positive correlation
Significance: Very significant (p << 0.001)

Rho interpretation: Weak negative correlation
Significance: Just significant (p < 0.05)

Significance: trend only, not statistically significant

p = 0.9745 (not significant)
ρ = -0.006 (zero)

Interpretation: No correlation at all

p = 0.0317 (significant)
ρ = 0.401 (moderate positive correlation)

Interpretation: Higher stroma scores associate with higher TIGIT expression

p = 0.0035 (significant)
ρ = 0.531 (strong positive correlation)

Interpretation: TSI strongly associates with higher TROP2 expression in tumor cells

p = 0.0092 (significant)
ρ = -0.478 (strong negative correlation)

Interpretation: Higher stroma scores associate with lower TROP2 expression

Thank you all!☺️
